tools.name: search_videos
tools.description:
Search for video segments using natural language. Returns relevant clips with timestamps, thumbnails, and extracted metadata.

Use this tool when the user wants to:
- Find specific content, moments, or topics in their video library
- Search for scenes matching a description
- Locate videos containing certain objects, actions, or concepts

The search uses semantic similarity, so natural language queries work best.

query.description: Natural language search query describing what to find

index_id.description: ID of the index to search. Use list_indexes first to discover available indexes.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100)

search_fields.description: Specific metadata fields to search (e.g., ["description", "objects"]). If not provided, searches all fields.

run_ids.description: Limit search to specific prompt run IDs. If not provided, searches across all runs.

index_ids.description: Optionally search across multiple indexes. If provided, backend uses these IDs instead of index_id path context.

tools.name: search_videos_by_image
tools.description:
Find video segments that are visually similar to a reference image.

Use this tool when the user has an image and wants to find similar content in their videos. The image must be provided as a base64-encoded string.

Returns segments ordered by visual similarity, including the matched frame timestamp.

image_data.description: Base64-encoded image data (without data URL prefix). Supports JPEG, PNG, WebP formats.

index_id.description: ID of the index to search

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100)

run_ids.description: Limit search to specific prompt run IDs. If not provided, searches across all runs.

index_ids.description: Optionally search across multiple indexes. If provided, backend uses these IDs instead of index_id path context.

tools.name: multimodal_search
tools.description:
Search using both text and image queries combined. Uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to merge results from both modalities.

Use this tool when the user wants to find content matching both:
- A text description (what the content is about)
- A visual reference (what it looks like)

You can adjust weights to prioritize text or image similarity.

index_id.description: ID of the index to search

text_query.description: Text search query (at least one of text_query or image_data required)

image_data.description: Base64-encoded image data (at least one of text_query or image_data required)

text_weight.description: Weight for text search results (0-1). Must sum to 1.0 with image_weight.

image_weight.description: Weight for image search results (0-1). Must sum to 1.0 with text_weight.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100)

search_fields.description: Specific metadata fields to search for text relevance (e.g., ["description", "objects"]).

run_ids.description: Limit search to specific prompt run IDs. If not provided, searches across all runs.

index_ids.description: Optionally search across multiple indexes. If provided, backend uses these IDs instead of index_id path context.

tools.name: filter_videos
tools.description:
Filter video segments by metadata field values using exact matches, ranges, or contains queries.

Use this tool for precise filtering when:
- You need exact matches (e.g., object_type = "vehicle")
- You need numeric ranges (e.g., confidence > 0.8)
- You need to check if an array contains a value

Supports up to 4 filter conditions combined with AND logic.

index_id.description: ID of the index to filter

field.description: Metadata field name to filter on

conditions.description: Filter conditions (1-4 conditions, combined with AND)

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous filter_videos response.

run_ids.description: Limit filtering to specific prompt runs.

index_ids.description: Optionally filter across multiple indexes. If provided, backend uses these IDs instead of index_id path context.

tools.name: list_indexes
tools.description:
List all video indexes the user has access to.

Use this tool to discover available video collections before searching. Returns both user-created indexes and shared demo indexes.

Each index contains videos that have been processed with specific prompts, enabling semantic search across their content.

tools.name: get_index
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a specific video index.

Returns the index name, creation date, and whether it's a shared demo index.

index_id.description: ID of the index to retrieve

tools.name: list_prompts
tools.description:
List available analysis prompts that can be used to process videos.

Prompts define what information to extract from videos (e.g., objects, actions, scenes). Each prompt has a JSON schema that defines the output structure.

Use this to discover what analysis capabilities are available before running execute_prompt.

tools.name: get_video
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a specific video.

Returns video metadata including title, overall status, media type, duration_seconds, timestamps, and per-prompt-run segment processing snapshots (pending/processing/successful/failed).

video_id.description: ID of the video to retrieve

tools.name: get_video_segments
tools.description:
Get all segments for a video with their extracted metadata.

Segments are time-based chunks of the video with associated metadata from prompt analysis. Use this after search to get full details about a video's content.

run_id.description: Specific prompt run ID to get results from

latest_run.description: Get results from the latest prompt run

limit.description: Maximum number of segments to return

tools.name: list_prompt_runs
tools.description:
List prompt runs for the current user, a specific index, or a specific video.

Use this to discover prior prompt executions before drilling into status, results, video-level summaries, billing settlement, or failed segments.

index_id.description: List prompt runs for a specific index. Cannot be combined with video_id.

video_id.description: List prompt runs that include a specific video. Cannot be combined with index_id.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of runs to return. User scope defaults to 200 and supports up to 200; index scope defaults to 50 and supports up to 100; video scope preserves full history when omitted.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. Only supported when index_id is provided.

tools.name: estimate_prompt_run
tools.description:
Estimate the billing cost of a prompt run using the same configuration as execution.

This does not start processing. Video/audio targets must already have server-measured duration metadata; otherwise the backend returns a duration validation error. Use it before execute_prompt when the user wants pricing or balance confirmation.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to estimate.

index_id.description: Required when target.type is index. Optional when target.type is videos.

video_segmentation_type.description: How to segment videos before extraction.

processing_model.description: Optional model override for prompt execution.

enable_transcription.description: Enable transcription for videos and audio.

enable_image_embedding.description: Enable image embeddings for image-search workflows.

tools.name: execute_prompt
tools.description:
Execute an analysis prompt against an index, specific videos, or playground media.

This starts an asynchronous processing job that analyzes videos with the specified prompt. The prompt defines what information to extract using its JSON schema.

Video/audio targets must already have server-measured duration metadata before execution. Returns immediately with a run_id. Use get_prompt_run_status to check progress.

Note: For large indexes, processing may take several minutes. Consider using webhooks for production workflows.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to execute. Use list_prompts to see available prompts.

index_id.description: Required when target.type is index. Optional when target.type is videos.

video_segmentation_type.description: How to segment videos: smart (scene detection), fixed (fixed duration), content_aware (pre-computed)

video_segment_duration.description: Duration in seconds for fixed segmentation (required if video_segmentation_type is "fixed")

audio_segment_duration.description: Duration in seconds for fixed audio segmentation (required if audio_segmentation_type is "fixed")

processing_model.description: Optional model override for prompt execution. Supported values are published by /billing/pricing processing_model_options.

enable_image_embedding.description: Enable image embeddings for visual search workflows.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of the prompt-run submission.

tools.name: get_prompt_run_status
tools.description:
Check the status and progress of a prompt execution run.

- Overall status (pending, processing, completed, completed_with_failures, failed, cancelled)
- Progress counts (completed/partial/failed/cancelled media and segments)
- Billing settlement fields (billing_status, billing_error, billing_estimated_mt, billing_actual_mt)
- Any error messages

Use this to monitor async processing jobs started with execute_prompt.

run_id.description: The prompt run ID returned from execute_prompt

tools.name: cancel_prompt_run
tools.description:
Request cancellation for a prompt run.

Already-started videos may finish, but no new videos will start after the stop is observed. Returned run data includes billing_status and billing_error so callers can distinguish released reservations from pending settlement or billable partial cancellation.

Use this when the user wants to stop an in-flight run because intent changed.

run_id.description: The prompt run ID returned from execute_prompt

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of the stop request.

tools.name: get_prompt_run_results
tools.description:
Get the extraction results from a completed prompt run.

Returns segment-level metadata extracted by the prompt's JSON schema. Only available after the run reaches a terminal status (completed or failed).

video_id.description: The video or audio media ID within the run.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: get_prompt_run_video_result
tools.description: Get the video/audio-level synthesis result for a specific media item inside a prompt run.

video_id.description: The video or audio media ID within the run.

tools.name: get_prompt_run_failed_segments
tools.description: Get the failed segment manifest for a prompt run grouped by video and operation.

tools.name: retry_prompt_run_segment
tools.description:
Retry a failed segment inside a completed prompt run without creating a new run.

Returns retry billing fields. Terminal retry states settle billing_status to confirmed or released unless billing_error indicates an unresolved owner-aware billing operation.

segment_id.description: The failed segment ID to retry.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry dispatch.

tools.name: get_prompt_run_segment_retry_status
tools.description:
Get the status of an async prompt-run segment retry.

Includes billing_status and billing_error for retry reservation settlement.

retry_id.description: The retry ID returned by retry_prompt_run_segment.

tools.name: create_prompt
tools.description:
Create a new custom extraction prompt with a JSON schema.

Prompts define what information to extract from video segments. The JSON schema specifies the structure of extracted metadata.

Use this tool when the user wants to:
- Define custom metadata fields to extract from videos
- Create a new analysis template for their specific use case
- Set up structured data extraction for their video library

The prompt_text is the instruction given to the AI model during extraction.
The json_schema must be a valid JSON Schema with root type "object".

name.description: A descriptive name for the prompt (3-100 characters)

description.description: Optional description of what this prompt extracts

prompt_text.description: The instruction text given to the AI model for extraction (10-5000 characters)

json_schema.description: JSON Schema defining the structure of extracted metadata.

semantic_indexing.description: Optional indexing controls for disabling specific segment or video-level fields.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of prompt creation.

tools.name: get_prompt
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a specific prompt including its full JSON schema.

Returns the complete prompt definition with:
- The prompt text (AI instruction)
- Full JSON schema for extraction
- Active status and creation date

Use this to understand what a prompt extracts before using it with execute_prompt.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to retrieve. Use list_prompts to discover available prompts.

tools.name: update_prompt
tools.description: Update an existing prompt's name, instructions, schemas, or video-level synthesis settings.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to update

json_schema.description: Updated JSON schema for segment extraction.

clear_video_level.description: Remove any existing video/audio-level synthesis configuration.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of prompt updates.

tools.name: test_prompt_schema
tools.description: Validate prompt JSON schema behavior against sample data using the backend schema validator.

sample_data.description: Sample payload to validate against the schema.

tools.name: get_prompt_usage
tools.description: Get usage statistics for a prompt, including whether it is active and still in use.

tools.name: create_gcs_connector
tools.description:
Create a Google Cloud Storage (GCS) connector for importing videos from cloud storage.

Requires a GCP service account with read access to the bucket. The credentials must be a valid service account key JSON.

After creating a connector, use test_connector to verify the connection and browse_connector_files to explore available files.

name.description: A descriptive name for this connector

gcp_project_id.description: Google Cloud project ID that owns the bucket

credentials_json.description: Service account key JSON object with fields like project_id, private_key, client_email, etc.

export_base_path.description: Base prefix allowed for export destination paths when export scope is enabled.

import_mode.description: Import dedupe behavior: all files or only new files.

idempotency_key.description: Required stable idempotency key for safe retry of connector creation.

tools.name: create_s3_connector
tools.description:
Create an Amazon S3 connector for importing videos from AWS cloud storage.

Requires AWS credentials with read access to the bucket (s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket permissions).

After creating a connector, use test_connector to verify the connection and browse_connector_files to explore available files.

name.description: A descriptive name for this connector

export_base_path.description: Base prefix allowed for export destination paths when export scope is enabled.

import_mode.description: Import dedupe behavior: all files or only new files.

idempotency_key.description: Required stable idempotency key for safe retry of connector creation.

tools.name: create_azure_connector
tools.description:
Create an Azure Blob Storage connector for importing videos from Azure cloud storage.

Requires an Azure service principal with Storage Blob Data Reader role on the container.

After creating a connector, use test_connector to verify the connection and browse_connector_files to explore available files.

name.description: A descriptive name for this connector

client_id.description: Azure service principal client ID (application ID)

export_base_path.description: Base prefix allowed for export destination paths when export scope is enabled.

import_mode.description: Import dedupe behavior: all files or only new files.

idempotency_key.description: Required stable idempotency key for safe retry of connector creation.

tools.name: list_connectors
tools.description:
List all cloud storage connectors configured for the user.

Returns connectors for all providers (GCS, S3, Azure) with their status and configuration details.

Use this to discover available connectors before importing files.

tools.name: test_connector
tools.description:
Test a cloud connector's connection to verify credentials and access.

Returns success/failure with error details if the connection fails. Use this after creating a connector or to diagnose connection issues.

connector_id.description: ID of the connector to test. Use list_connectors to find connector IDs.

tools.name: browse_connector_files
tools.description:
Browse files in a cloud storage connector.

Returns a list of files matching the specified prefix and pattern. Use this to discover available video files before creating an import job.

Supports glob patterns for filtering (e.g., "*.mp4", "videos/*.mov").

connector_id.description: ID of the connector to browse

tools.name: create_import_job
tools.description:
Create and start an import job to bring files from a cloud connector into an index.

This starts an asynchronous job that imports video/audio/image files from your cloud storage (GCS, S3, or Azure) into a VideoVector index.

Use browse_connector_files first to discover available files, then create an import job with the desired prefix and pattern.

Returns immediately with a job_id. Use get_import_job to check progress.

connector_id.description: ID of the cloud connector to import from. Use list_connectors to find available connectors.

index_id.description: Target index ID where imported videos will appear. Use list_indexes or create_index.

file_pattern.description: Glob pattern for files to import (e.g., "*.mp4", "*.{mp4,mov,avi}")

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of import-job creation.

tools.name: list_import_jobs
tools.description:
List all import jobs for the user.

Returns jobs with their status, progress, and results. Use this to monitor ongoing imports or review past import history.

tools.name: get_import_job
tools.description:
Get detailed status and progress of a specific import job.

Returns:
- Current status (pending, scanning, importing, completed, failed, cancelled)
- Progress (total files, imported, failed, skipped, bytes transferred)
- List of imported video IDs
- Details of any failed or skipped files

job_id.description: ID of the import job. Use list_import_jobs to find job IDs.

tools.name: cancel_import_job
tools.description:
Cancel a running import job.

Files already imported will remain in the index. Only pending files will be skipped.

Cannot cancel jobs that are already completed, failed, or cancelled.

job_id.description: ID of the import job to cancel

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of import-job cancellation.

tools.name: create_index
tools.description:
Create a new video index.

Indexes are collections that hold videos and their processed metadata. Create an index before uploading videos or running imports.

After creating an index, you can:
- Import videos from cloud storage using create_import_job
- Run prompts on the index using execute_prompt
- Search the index using search_videos

name.description: Name for the new index (1-255 characters)

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of index creation.

tools.name: list_videos
tools.description:
List videos in an index with pagination.

Returns video metadata including title, overall status, and run-based segment processing summaries (without loading segment payloads), plus media type and timestamps. Use this to browse the contents of an index.

index_id.description: ID of the index to list videos from

limit.description: Maximum number of videos to return (1-100)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from previous response for next page

tools.name: get_videos_status
tools.description:
Get processing status for multiple videos at once.

Efficiently check the processing state of up to 100 videos in a single call. Returns both overall video status and run-based segment status snapshots for each video.

Use this to monitor video processing progress after imports or uploads.

video_ids.description: List of video IDs to check status for (1-100 videos)

tools.name: export_index_metadata
tools.description:
Export all extracted metadata from an index to a downloadable file.

Creates an asynchronous export job that compiles all video segments and their extracted metadata into a JSON file.

Optionally filter to specific prompt runs. Returns immediately with an export_id - use get_export_status to check when the file is ready.

index_id.description: ID of the index to export

prompt_run_ids.description: Optional: specific prompt run IDs to include. If not provided, exports all runs.

destination_connector_id.description: Optional connector ID for cloud-destination export instead of a downloadable file.

destination_subpath.description: Optional sub-prefix under the connector export base path.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of export creation.

tools.name: export_prompt_run
tools.description:
Export metadata from a specific prompt run to a downloadable file.

Creates an asynchronous export job for the results of a single prompt execution. Returns immediately with an export_id - use get_export_status to check when the file is ready.

run_id.description: ID of the prompt run to export

destination_connector_id.description: Optional connector ID for cloud-destination export instead of a downloadable file.

destination_subpath.description: Optional sub-prefix under the connector export base path.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of export creation.

tools.name: get_export_status
tools.description:
Get the status and delivery details of an export job.

Returns:
- status: processing, completed, or failed
- destination_type: direct download or connector delivery
- destination_connector_id and destination_uri: connector delivery details when applicable
- download_url: authenticated API download endpoint for a completed direct export
- file_size_bytes: size of the exported file
- error_message: details if the export failed

This tool is side-effect free and never creates or returns a bearer capability.
Use the authenticated VideoVector SDK/API streaming download for large files.
Call get_export_download_url only when a separate header-free client explicitly
needs a short-lived bounded bearer URL.

export_id.description: ID of the export job to check

tools.name: get_export_download_url
tools.description:
Explicitly mint a bounded bearer URL for one owned export.

Returns exactly:
- export_id
- status
- destination_type: download or connector
- destination_connector_id
- download_url: short-lived bearer URL, or null when not downloadable

This operation creates a fresh bearer capability and is not idempotent. It
returns download_url=null for processing, failed, connector-delivered, and
otherwise unavailable exports. Treat any non-null URL as sensitive: do not log,
persist, or share it beyond the intended client. Prefer authenticated SDK/API
streaming for large files instead of loading export content into MCP context.

export_id.description: ID of the owned export for which to mint a bounded URL

tools.name: create_webhook
tools.description:
Create a webhook to receive real-time notifications for events.

Webhooks send HTTP POST requests to your endpoint when events occur (e.g., video processing completed, prompt run finished).

The URL must use HTTPS. A signing secret is returned once at creation - store it securely to verify webhook signatures.

Use list_webhook_events first to see available event types.

events.description: Event types to subscribe to. Use list_webhook_events to see available events.

index_ids.description: Filter to specific indexes (optional, omit for all indexes)

metadata.description: Custom metadata to include with each delivery (optional)

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of webhook creation.

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description:
List all webhooks configured for the user.

Returns webhook configurations including their status, subscribed events, and failure counts.

tools.name: get_webhook
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a specific webhook.

Returns the full webhook configuration including events, status, and delivery statistics.

webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook to retrieve

tools.name: update_webhook
tools.description:
Update a webhook's configuration.

Can update the name, URL, subscribed events, index filters, status (active/paused), or metadata.

At least one field must be provided.

webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook to update

events.description: New list of event types to subscribe to

index_ids.description: New list of index IDs to filter to

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of webhook updates.

tools.name: list_webhook_events
tools.description:
List all supported webhook event types.

Returns the available events you can subscribe to when creating or updating webhooks.

Common events include:
- media.processing.completed - Video/audio/image finished processing
- prompt_run.completed / prompt_run.cancelled - Prompt execution reached a terminal state
- import_job.completed - Import job finished

tools.name: test_webhook
tools.description:
Send a test event to verify webhook configuration.

Sends a test payload to the webhook URL to verify it's reachable and correctly configured.

Returns success/failure with the HTTP status code or error message.

webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook to test

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retry of webhook test dispatch.

tools.name: list_webhook_deliveries
tools.description:
List delivery attempts for a webhook.

Returns the history of webhook deliveries including status, attempts, response codes, and any errors.

Use this to debug webhook issues or verify events are being delivered.

webhook_id.description: ID of the webhook to get deliveries for

limit.description: Maximum number of deliveries to return
